title: beilia spicata var. triticea
intro: Beilia spicata var. triticea, also known as Bupleurum, is a flowering plant native to Asia, belonging to the family Apiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of sesquiterpenoids and alkaloids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and immunomodulatory effects. Beilia spicata var. triticea has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, liver and kidney diseases, and respiratory issues, thanks to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and promoting liver detoxification. Herbal preparations of Beilia spicata var. triticea include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine and modern herbalism.
